opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter long on first intraday pullback to 148-154 following the after-hours gap up; look for volume confirmation on the retest.
opportunities.rationale: Oracle beat Q3 FY26 estimates decisively: EPS $1.79 vs $1.71 est, revenue $17.19B vs $16.91B est, cloud revenue +44% YoY. Remaining performance obligations surged 325% to $553B. Stock gapped ~10% to ~$149-152. Classic gap-follow-through setup — enter on intraday pullback to the gap zone, targeting continuation toward prior highs around $172.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on dip to 272-280 if price holds above 265 on 11-12 Mar intraday; EXIT ALL POSITIONS BEFORE CLOSE ON 12 MAR.
opportunities.rationale: Adobe reports Q1 FY2026 on 12 March after close. Consensus: ~9.9% YoY revenue growth; Citi PT lowered to $315 (Neutral); RBC Outperform at $430. Stock trading at $282, down from $387 Citi prior PT, implying high bar for positive surprise. Pre-earnings run-up trade — capture premium expansion and any upward drift into the print. CRITICAL: exit entirely before market close 12 Mar to avoid binary earnings risk.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter on consolidation near 218-225 on 11 Mar; exit before 12 Mar close.
opportunities.rationale: Dick's reporting Thursday 12 Mar before open. Consumer discretionary spending has held up despite macro noise; analyst expectations moderate. Pre-earnings drift trade with exit before print to avoid binary risk.

opportunities.entry_trigger: Enter near 150-156 if homebuilder sector holds up; exit before 12 Mar close.
opportunities.rationale: Lennar reporting Thursday 12 Mar. Homebuilder segment faces macro headwinds (elevated mortgage rates), but strong order data may support a pre-earnings drift. Low conviction given rate sensitivity; small size only.
